prompt.content:
You are a workflow automation agent. Execute the requested task using the available tools.
- Do not ask clarifying questions. Take action instead.
- Referenced data (spreadsheets, policies, guidelines, rosters) exists in the simulated environment — discover it by searching email, listing spreadsheets, querying calendars, etc. If the prompt says 'our current X policy' or 'the Y guidelines,' search for it.
- Never respond with a list of missing information.

Go through our recent Twitter mentions and handle them per the social engagement SOP. Make sure you're following the latest guidelines before starting.

Today is January 27, 2026. Include relevant counts in your message(s).

tweets.text: @OurBrand Just upgraded to the pro plan and loving it! The new features are amazing.
@OurBrand Does your product integrate with Salesforce? Looking for a solution for my team.
@OurBrand Your app has been down for 2 hours! This is costing us money. Unacceptable!
Just saw an ad for @OurBrand, might check it out later
@OurBrand is hands down the best tool I've used for productivity. Been recommending it to everyone!
@OurBrand vs Competitor X - both have their strengths honestly. Hard to pick.
RT @DataDrivenDsgn: @OurBrand is hands down the best tool I've used for productivity.

messages.subject: Social Engagement SOP - Q1 2026
messages.body_plain:
Updated social engagement guidelines:

1. POSITIVE mentions (praise, recommendations, upgrades): Like the tweet.
2. QUESTIONS (product inquiries, feature requests): Reply with a helpful answer.
3. COMPLAINTS (outages, bugs, service issues): Do NOT reply publicly. Escalate to #support-alerts in Slack with the username and issue summary. Do NOT like complaints.
4. NEUTRAL mentions (casual references, ads seen): No action needed - skip these.
5. COMPETITOR COMPARISONS: Do not engage - skip these entirely.
6. RETWEETS of existing mentions: Do not like or reply to retweets.

Important: When escalating to Slack, always include the Twitter username so the support team can follow up directly.
